overview: From 2026-05-19 to 2026-05-20, elizaos/eliza had 30 new PRs (17 merged), 1 new issues, and 10 active contributors.

topIssues.title: cloud-api: non-streaming /v1/chat/completions returns 500 when the ai_billing_records insert fails
topIssues.author: jqmwa
topIssues.body:
## Summary

A non-streaming `POST /api/v1/chat/completions` against Eliza Cloud currently fails for every request. The model inference succeeds and credits are reconciled, but the request still returns **HTTP 500** because the durable `ai_billing_records` audit-row insert throws — and the non-streaming handler treats that as fatal.

Three issues are bundled here:

1. The non-streaming handler treats an audit-record write failure as fatal — **inconsistent with the streaming path**, which swallows the identical failure.
2. The `ai_billing_records` INSERT fails — the underlying Postgres cause is not surfaced, so it can't be diagnosed from outside.
3. The API leaks raw SQL + bind params (including internal UUIDs) in the error body.

## Environment

- Eliza Cloud — `https://www.elizacloud.ai/api/v1/chat/completions`
- Non-streaming (`stream` unset / `false`)
- Model-independent — reproduced with `openai/gpt-4o-mini`, `anthropic/claude-haiku-4.5`, `google/gemini-2.5-flash`, `google/gemini-2.0-flash-001`
- 100% reproducible, including the first request on a freshly-funded org

## Reproduction

```bash
curl -sS https://www.elizacloud.ai/api/v1/chat/completions \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer <API_KEY>" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"model":"openai/gpt-4o-mini","messages":[{"role":"user","content":"hi"}]}'
```

Response (HTTP 500), with the org/user UUIDs redacted:

```json
{"error":{"message":"Failed query: insert into \"ai_billing_records\" (\"id\", \"organization_id\", \"user_id\", \"usage_record_id\", \"reservation_transaction_id\", \"settlement_transaction_ids\", \"idempotency_key\", \"request_id\", \"provider\", \"model\", \"billing_source\", \"pricing_snapshot_ids\", \"provider_request_id\", \"provider_instance_id\", \"provider_endpoint\", \"usage_total_cost\", \"ledger_total\", \"status\", \"metadata\", \"created_at\") values (default, $1, ... $18, default) on conflict (\"organization_id\",\"idempotency_key\") do nothing returning ...\nparams: <org_id>,<user_id>,<usage_record_id>,<reservation_txn_id>,[\"<settlement_txn_id>\"],<idempotency_key>,<request_id>,openai,openai/gpt-4o-mini,openrouter,[],,,,0.000007,0.000007,recorded,{...}","type":"api_error"}}
```

The `metadata` param shows billing already completed before this insert: `"reservation":{"reservedAmount":0.00054,"actualCost":0.000007,"adjustmentType":"refund"}` — inference and credit settlement both succeeded.

## Analysis

### 1. Non-streaming handler aborts on audit-row failure

In `packages/cloud-api/v1/chat/completions/route.ts`, `handleNonStreamingRequest()`:

```ts
try {
  const result = await generateText({ ... });                       // inference OK
  const billing = await billUsage(billingContext, result.usage);
  const reconciliation = await settleReservation(billing.totalCost); // credits settled
  ...
  const usageRecord = await recordUsageAnalytics(billingContext, billing, { ... });
  if (usageRecord) {
    await aiBillingRecordsService.record({ ... });                   // <-- throws
  }
  ...
  return addCorsHeaders(Response.json({ /* completion */ }));
} catch (error) {
  await settleReservation(0);   // re-settles an already-settled reservation
  throw error;                  // -> 500, the completed response is discarded
}
```

When `aiBillingRecordsService.record()` throws:

- the already-generated completion is discarded and the caller gets a 500;
- `settleReservation(0)` runs a **second time**, after `settleReservation(billing.totalCost)` already ran for the same reservation.

The **streaming** path handles the identical call differently — `streamText({ onFinish })` wraps `aiBillingRecordsService.record()` in its own `try/catch` that logs and continues:

```ts
onFinish: async ({ text, usage }) => {
  try {
    ...
    if (usageRecord) {
      await aiBillingRecordsService.record({ ... });
    }
    ...
  } catch (error) {
    await settleReservation(0);
    logger.error("[Chat Completions] onFinish error", { ... });
  }
}
```

The schema doc comment describes `ai_billing_records` as a reconciliation/audit join record, so the streaming behavior (log + continue) looks like the intended one. The non-streaming path should not fail a request — and discard a paid-for completion — because an audit row didn't persist.

### 2. The INSERT itself fails (root cause not visible)

`AiBillingRecordsRepository.createDeduped()` in `packages/cloud-shared/src/db/repositories/ai-billing-records.ts`:

```ts
.insert(aiBillingRecords).values(data)
.onConflictDoNothing({ target: [aiBillingRecords.organization_id, aiBillingRecords.idempotency_key] })
.returning();
```

The API surfaces Drizzle's `Failed query: ...` wrapper message but not the underlying `pg` error (`error.cause`), so the real reason is invisible from outside. Since it is 100% reproducible from the first request, plausible causes:

- the `ai_billing_records_org_idempotency_unique` unique index is missing in the deployed DB, so `ON CONFLICT ("organization_id","idempotency_key")` matches no constraint (Postgres `42P10`). Migration `0118_add_ai_billing_and_alert_events.sql` creates it with `CREATE UNIQUE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS`, so a partially-applied migration would produce exactly this.
- a foreign-key violation on `usage_record_id` or `reservation_transaction_id` if the referenced row isn't visible to the writer connection.

Logging `error.cause` alongside the Drizzle wrapper would make this self-diagnosable.

### 3. Information disclosure

The error response returns the full SQL statement, every bind parameter, and internal UUIDs (organization_id, user_id, usage_record_id, transaction IDs) to the API client. DB-layer errors should be logged server-side and returned to clients as a generic message.

## Impact

Non-streaming chat completions are currently unusable on Eliza Cloud — every call 500s after the model has already run and credits have been settled.

topPRs.title: fix(os): polish elizaOS live demo
topPRs.author: NubsCarson
topPRs.body:
## Summary

This keeps the elizaOS Live / USB-demo branch current with `develop` and hardens the parts that were breaking fresh installs, package-mode validation, mobile packaging, live CI validation, and the Linux live demo surface.

- Polishes the elizaOS Live visual path across greeter, GNOME shell/window list, startup/failure shell, and voice onboarding.
- Keeps the bundled elizaOS/Milady app as the auto-starting home surface while preserving normal GNOME/Tails desktop use.
- Keeps the broken voice pill path opt-in until the pill renderer has a production UI.
- Hardens the USB installer write flow, backend contracts, startup-shell fallback assets, package-mode UI/test wiring, and runtime packaging checks.
- Carries the current local-inference/native merge state, including the latest `elizaOS/llama.cpp` submodule tip used by this branch.
- Fixes latest `develop` merge fallout around duplicate package exports, the Capacitor llama patcher/patch conflict, voice diarizer/phone package conflicts, Android mobile ONNX sidecars, duplicate SMS gateway package scripts, lockfile drift, formatting drift, and the local-inference CI build path.
- Adds the missing remote-capability GitHub live artifact validation path so manual/nightly live runs download and validate both Cloud and provider evidence artifacts.

## Current Source Base

Updated 2026-05-19 with normal merges, not rebases.

- PR head: `d3eb80c11ebeb72739a9ad59cada31b8a682ed9f`
- Latest merged `origin/develop`: `d32b6a446185e77267f1e51005a1796de244b43b`
- `plugins/plugin-local-inference/native/llama.cpp`: `96dd1a8466c84bdd419faf3866425260623fb6b0`
- Branch was pushed normally; no force-push was used for this refresh.
- GitHub currently reports `mergeStateStatus: UNSTABLE` because checks are rerunning on the latest pushed head.

## Latest Local Validation

Fresh local validation after merging latest `develop` and resolving the local-inference build-path conflict to upstream's cleaner `bun run build.ts` implementation:

```bash
bun install --frozen-lockfile
bun run --cwd plugins/plugin-local-inference build
bun run --cwd plugins/plugin-local-inference format:check
git diff --check
```

Results:

- Frozen install passed; postinstall reported `patch-llama-cpp-capacitor patched=0 already-applied=1 repaired=0 failed=0`.
- `@elizaos/plugin-local-inference` build passed using `bun run build.ts`, avoiding the previous CI failure from a bare `tsup` binary.
- `@elizaos/plugin-local-inference` format check passed: 339 files checked, no fixes applied.
- PR delta whitespace check passed.

Additional validation from the immediately preceding merge head:

```bash
bun run build:core
```

Result: 38/38 build tasks passed, including `@elizaos/plugin-local-inference`.

Earlier validation retained from this PR after previous latest-`develop` merges:

```bash
bun run format:check
bun run test:remote-capabilities
bun run test:remote-capabilities:live-ci-audit
bun run test:remote-capabilities:live-ci-audit:self-test
bun run --cwd packages/agent build:mobile
node --test packages/app-core/scripts/run-mobile-build-policy.test.mjs
node --check packages/scripts/audit-capability-router-live-ci.ts
node --check packages/scripts/audit-capability-router-live-ci.self-test.ts
```

Results:

- Full workspace format check passed: 75 tasks successful.
- Remote capability suite passed: 9 files, 190 tests passed, 3 skipped.
- Live CI audit passed: 45 checks.
- Live CI audit self-test passed: 45 checks.
- Android mobile bundle builds after the OmniVoice/ONNX cleanup and generated CSS-string step; bundle output no longer requires ONNX sidecars.
- Mobile build policy node:test passed: 24 tests.
- Live CI audit scripts parse cleanly under `node --check`.

Earlier validation retained from this PR:

```bash
bunx vitest run --config vitest.config.ts \
  plugins/plugin-local-inference/__tests__/voice-speaker-diarizer.test.ts \
  plugins/plugin-local-inference/__tests__/voice-speaker-encoder.test.ts \
  plugins/plugin-local-inference/src/services/engine.voice.test.ts \
  plugins/plugin-local-inference/src/services/voice/whisper-cpp-asr.test.ts
bun run verify:cloud
bun run --cwd packages/os/usb-installer test
bun packages/agent/scripts/build-mobile-bundle.mjs --target=ios
bun --config=/tmp/bunfig-no-coverage.toml test \
  plugins/plugin-aosp-local-inference/__tests__/aosp-kokoro-tts-handler.test.ts \
  plugins/plugin-aosp-local-inference/__tests__/aosp-local-inference-bootstrap.test.ts \
  --reporter=dots
```

Results: USB installer validation passed locally on the current branch lineage: unit/integration tests (9 files, 76 pass, 1 opt-in virtual-device skip), typecheck, build, expanded Biome lint over src/tests/server/config, Playwright e2e (6 pass across desktop/mobile mocked browser flows), and the guarded Linux virtual USB proof (scsi_debug block device, sudo -n dd write, readback SHA-256, cleanup verified). Earlier voice/local-inference, cloud verification, mobile bundle, and AOSP OmniVoice/TTS checks remain documented here but were not rerun after the final merge.

Earlier artifact validation from this PR remains documented here, but it was not re-run after the final `develop` merge:

- Built ISO: `packages/os/linux/variants/milady-tails/out/binary.iso`
- Validated ISO SHA256: `3a26c4990a5dcc53c4a5f3dcf3e6c400a0df5a06aeb6cf37c5fb03c210dfb9f2`
- QEMU visual validation confirmed the elizaOS greeter, light branding, normal GNOME desktop, app auto-launch, white/blue onboarding, and no broken pill-loader takeover.
- `./scripts/static-smoke.sh`, `./scripts/security-smoke.sh`, and `./scripts/runtime-api-smoke.sh` were previously run for that artifact.

## Current GitHub Status

- PR is draft while CI re-runs on `d3eb80c11e`.
- The previous red `Server Tests` failure was reproduced locally and fixed by adding the missing GitHub live artifact validation workflow path.
- The later `tsup: command not found` CI failure was addressed by merging upstream's `plugin-local-inference` `build.ts` fix and validating the package build locally.
- Current checks are queued/running on the new head; skipped fork/security/manual gates are expected.

## Remaining Hardware/Product Gaps

Before making hardware/product claims, still needed:

- Repeat guarded physical USB flash/readback for a fresh ISO built from the current head. The Linux fake-media and scsi_debug virtual block-device E2Es prove the guarded server/backend write path safely, including real Linux dd/readback, but they are not a hardware boot substitute.
- Boot that USB on real hardware.
- Validate real USB Persistent Storage create/unlock/delete behavior.
- Validate privacy/direct networking behavior for app, renderer, embedded browser, OAuth, and external web surfaces.
- Production release still needs real update keyring, revocation metadata, downloader UX, rollback health policy, SBOM, provenance, and formal inherited Tails sudoers review.

topPRs.title: ci(flatpak): commit node-sources.json + flip elizaos-app to --offline (Flathub-ready)
topPRs.author: 2-A-M
topPRs.body:
## Summary Completes the Flathub-readiness work begun in #7787. That PR added the `generate-sources.sh` script and wired it into `test-flatpak.yml` so CI regenerates `node-sources.json` on every run and uploads it as the `flatpak-node-sources` artifact. This PR finishes the flip: - **Commits `node-sources.json`** (691 KB, 1202 entries) next to the manifest so Flathub's network-less builder can reproduce the install offline. Generated against the current `elizaos@latest` (`1.7.2`) dependency closure. - **Flips `ai.elizaos.App.yml`** (direct/power-user variant): the `elizaos-app` module now runs `npm install -g --offline --prefix=/app --ignore-scripts elizaos`, and `build-options.build-args: --share=network` is gone. The build phase no longer needs network access, which is exactly what Flathub's submission pipeline requires. - **Rewrites the long-form comment block** above the `elizaos-app` module — the 3-step Flathub-prep playbook from #7787 is no longer aspirational; it's how the manifest works today. - **README**: rewrites the "Flathub submission checklist" step 1 to point at the now-automated CI artifact path. ### Bug fix in `generate-sources.sh` While generating the artifact, hit a real bug in the helper script: `flatpak-node-generator 0.1.1` crashes with `KeyError: 'name'` when it parses the root `packages.""` entry of the lockfile, because `npm install --package-lock-only elizaos@latest` writes a `package.json` with only a `dependencies` block (no `name` field). Fix: seed a minimal `package.json` with `"name": "elizaos-flatpak-sources-shim"` before invoking `npm install`. The generator now reads the root entry cleanly and emits all 1202 source records. Without this fix the CI `Generate offline node sources` step would silently `continue-on-error`-fail forever and the artifact would never appear in upload-artifact's output — which is exactly the state we were in before this PR. ### After this lands The direct manifest builds with no network access during `build`, which is the Flathub submission requirement. Next step (separate PR, separate repo): open `flathub/flathub` submission issue for `ai.elizaos.App`. ## Test plan - [ ] `Test Flatpak Build` workflow goes green on this PR with the committed `node-sources.json` and `--offline` install path. - [ ] `Generate offline node sources` CI step succeeds (verifies the `KeyError: 'name'` fix on the runner's `flatpak-node-generator` version). - [ ] `appstream-util validate-relax` and `desktop-file-validate` still pass (already covered by the workflow). - [ ] `flatpak run ai.elizaos.App --version` still succeeds inside the sandboxed build. Verified locally on WSL Ubuntu 24.04 + Node 18 + `flatpak-node-generator 0.1.1` (uv tool install): script generates 691 KB / 1202-entry `node-sources.json` against `elizaos@1.7.2` with the fix; crashes deterministically with `KeyError: 'name'` without it.

Greptile Summary

This PR completes the Flathub-readiness work for the elizaOS App Flatpak by committing a `node-sources.json` manifest (1202 entries, elizaos@1.7.2) and flipping the `elizaos-app` module to a fully offline build. - **`ai.elizaos.App.yml`**: Removes `--share=network` from build options, adds `npm_config_cache` pointing at the vendored cache, switches npm to `--offline --ignore-scripts`, and correctly references `node-sources.json` as a bare-string include so flatpak-builder processes its archive/script entries rather than treating it as an opaque file. - **`generate-sources.sh`**: Fixes the `KeyError: 'name'` crash in `flatpak-node-generator 0.1.1` by seeding a minimal `package.json` before `npm install --package-lock-only`; however, the end-of-run hint still prints the now-incorrect `- type: file` snippet instead of the bare-string `- node-sources.json` syntax used in the manifest. - **`README.md` + `node-sources.json`**: Checklist and committed source manifest updated to reflect the automated CI workflow and offline build capability.

Confidence Score: 4/5

The manifest changes are correctly applied, but the generate-sources.sh hint message directs future maintainers to use the old broken `type: file` syntax, which would silently regress the offline build if followed. The offline build wiring in the manifest is correct and addresses all prior review findings. The one remaining defect is in generate-sources.sh: the end-of-run help text still instructs users to add `- type: file / path: node-sources.json`, which would cause a regression if a future maintainer regenerates and re-integrates the file by following that prompt. packages/app-core/packaging/flatpak/generate-sources.sh — the trailing hint message needs to be updated to match the bare-string syntax now used in the manifest.

Important Files Changed

| Filename | Overview | |----------|----------| | packages/app-core/packaging/flatpak/ai.elizaos.App.yml | Removes --share=network build-arg, adds npm_config_cache env var pointing at the vendored cache directory, switches npm install to --offline --ignore-scripts, and changes node-sources.json from type: file to bare-string include — correctly implementing the offline Flathub-ready build. | | packages/app-core/packaging/flatpak/generate-sources.sh | Fixes the KeyError: 'name' bug by seeding package.json before npm install --package-lock-only, but the script's end-of-run hint message still instructs users to use the now-incorrect `- type: file` syntax instead of the bare-string `- node-sources.json`. | | packages/app-core/packaging/flatpak/README.md | Updates Flathub submission checklist step 1 to describe the automated CI artifact path and the now-committed node-sources.json; content is accurate. | | packages/app-core/packaging/flatpak/node-sources.json | Adds the 691 KB / 1202-entry flatpak-node-generator manifest for elizaos@1.7.2; pinned to a specific version and will need to be refreshed on each new elizaos release, as documented in the README and CI workflow. |

Flowchart

```mermaid %%{init: {'theme': 'neutral'}}%% flowchart TD A[generate-sources.sh] -->|npm install --package-lock-only elizaos@latest| B[package-lock.json] B -->|flatpak-node-generator npm --recursive| C[node-sources.json\n691 KB / 1202 entries] C -->|committed to repo| D[ai.elizaos.App.yml\nbuild-time source] D -->|bare-string include| E{flatpak-builder\nprocesses entries} E -->|archive entries| F[flatpak-node/npm-cache/\nvendored tarballs] E -->|script/shell entries| G[setup scripts] F -->|npm_config_cache points here| H[npm install -g --offline\n--prefix=/app --ignore-scripts elizaos] G --> H H -->|no network needed| I[elizaos installed in /app] I --> J[Flathub-submittable build] ```

Comments Outside Diff (1)

 1. `packages/app-core/packaging/flatpak/generate-sources.sh`, line 72-74 ([link](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/blob/4deb0d708ea1fabe5d90ab51cf4260d22cbc8514/packages/app-core/packaging/flatpak/generate-sources.sh#L72-L74)) The hint message at the end of the script still instructs users to add `- type: file` syntax for `node-sources.json`, but the manifest now uses the bare-string syntax (`- node-sources.json`) so that flatpak-builder processes the file as a JSON source manifest rather than copying it as an opaque file. Following this printed guidance would silently regress the fix introduced in this PR.
Reviews (3): Last reviewed commit: ["fix(flatpak): set npm\_config\_cache so --..."](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/commit/4deb0d708ea1fabe5d90ab51cf4260d22cbc8514) | [Re-trigger Greptile](https://app.greptile.com/api/retrigger?id=32804491)

topPRs.title: Shell foundation v1 + onboarding a11y/contrast pass
topPRs.author: rndrntwrk
topPRs.body: ## Summary Two pieces of work bundled on `enoomian/shell-foundation` (28 commits off `shaw/eliza-app-release-blockers`): **1. Onboarding accessibility + contrast pass** (audit-driven, 9 fix commits) - Documented audit of every screen in the `VoicePrefixGate` flow → Hello launcher → Setup screen, with severity-tagged issue IDs. See `docs/apps/desktop/2026-05-16-onboarding-ux-audit.md`. - Removed a `text-black` class on the pre-agent shell wrapper that was rendering every step's heading + body paragraphs as black-on-near-black (WCAG fail). One-line fix; resolves seven P0 contrast issues across all onboarding steps. - Removed a hardcoded `?? "Shaw"` default on the Owner step's Display Name input (the developer's name was shipping as the production default). - Defaulted the shared `

topPRs.title: feat(cloud): all agent lifecycle ops via job queue (Workers don't call cores)
topPRs.author: standujar
topPRs.body:
> **Note**: this PR supersedes [#7808](https://github.com/elizaOS/eliza/pull/7808), which was auto-closed when the branch was renamed (\`feat/orchestrator-http-server\` → \`feat/agent-lifecycle-via-job-queue\`). The first three commits (\`d29da4c\`, \`fb1a92b\`, \`7f670b9\`) are identical to what reviewers saw on #7808 — including the Greptile findings and the fix commit \`7f670b9\`. ## Architectural shift **Cloudflare Workers never talk to Hetzner cores directly.** Every cores-bound op flows through the job queue, the orchestrator daemon (the only context with SSH keys + a stable outbound network identity) executes it, and the Worker reads the result from \`jobs.result\`. Previously the Worker code path called \`elizaSandboxService.shutdown()\` / \`provision()\` / \`snapshot()\` / bridge-HTTP-fetch inline. SSH-bound calls silently no-op'd from Workers; bridge-HTTP calls failed for stopped or crashed agents (no bridge to fetch from). Result: DB rows that claimed \`stopped\` while containers kept burning RAM, "save snapshot" 500s, and a logs view that returned empty for any non-trivially-running agent. ## Scope (all shipped in this PR) Six lifecycle operations, five new job types, six route rewrites. Pattern mirrors the shipped \`agent_delete\` (PR #7746). | Op | Job type | Daemon executor | Route(s) | |---|---|---|---| | Suspend | \`AGENT_SUSPEND\` | \`executeSuspend\` — SSH \`docker stop\`, DB→stopped, keeps sandbox_id | \`PATCH /v1/eliza/agents/[id]\`, \`POST /v1/eliza/agents/[id]/suspend\`, \`POST /v1/agents/[id]/suspend\`, \`POST /compat/agents/[id]/suspend\` | | Resume | \`AGENT_RESUME\` | \`executeResume\` → \`provision()\` (URLs + DB restored) | \`POST /v1/eliza/agents/[id]/resume\` | | Restart | \`AGENT_RESTART\` | \`executeRestart\` — shutdown + provision atomic | \`POST /v1/agents/[id]/restart\` | | Logs | \`AGENT_LOGS\` | \`executeLogs\` — SSH \`docker logs --tail N\` via \`provider.fetchLogs()\` | \`GET /v1/agents/[id]/logs\`, \`GET /compat/agents/[id]/logs\` | | Snapshot | \`AGENT_SNAPSHOT\` | \`executeSnapshot\` — daemon-side snapshot() | \`POST /v1/eliza/agents/[id]/snapshot\` | ## Greptile-resolved (from #7808, commit \`7f670b9\`) The fast-path \`docker start\` in \`executeResume\` was dead code on \`DockerSandboxProvider\` (no standalone \`start()\` exposed), so every resume already paid the full re-provision cost. Greptile flagged three derived issues: bridge_url not restored, no lifecycle lock, silent fallback. Fix: drop the fast path. \`provision()\` restores URLs and holds its own advisory lock. Real fast path returns when the provider grows a \`start(sandboxId): Promise\` — tracked as follow-up. ## What I tested **Locally:** - [x] \`tsc --noEmit\` clean on cloud-shared (touched files filtered) - [x] biome check clean on all 10 touched files - [x] \`bun test packages/cloud-shared/src/lib/services/__tests__/provisioning-job-types.test.ts\` → 4 pass, 100% coverage on the registry file. Catches typos / missing entries / duplicates / non-snake-case. - [x] Manual /clean single-pass on the diff: no slop, no defensive overkill, no \`any\` / \`as unknown\` casts. JSDocs explain Why. **What I did NOT test (needs deploy on both Worker + daemon VM):** - [ ] e2e dashboard: each of suspend / resume / restart / logs / snapshot via the dashboard against a live agent - [ ] Idempotency: rapid double-click on any op → second returns existing job - [ ] Logs job uniqueness: completed logs job is NOT reused on a fresh click (only in-flight ones are) - [ ] \`/api/v1/jobs/\` polling surfaces status transitions correctly **Test gap:** The \`execute*\` methods aren't unit-tested. cloud-shared has no harness for mocking \`dbWrite.transaction\` + advisory locks + SSH provider. Same gap as the shipped \`agent_delete\` / \`agent_provision\` paths. Follow-up of its own size. ## Frontend UX note Snapshot / suspend / restart toasts fire optimistically (the same as today for the already-shipped suspend route): the user sees "Snapshot saved" before the job actually completes. The DB eventually reflects reality. To make the toast truthful, the frontend needs to extend its existing \`poller.track(agentId, jobId)\` flow (used today for provision/resume) to cover the new 202 paths. Follow-up frontend PR. ## Follow-up - Frontend poller for the new 202 routes (suspend, restart, snapshot, logs) — kills the optimistic-toast lie. - Real fast-path \`docker start\` via \`provider.start()\` returning a fresh handle (perf optimization, ~5s vs ~60s for resume). - DRY the now-seven \`enqueue*Once\` methods — ~80 LOC of near-identical lock + idempotency code per method. Touches shipped code, so out of scope here. - Test harness for the execute* methods (covers this PR + the existing \`agent_delete\` / \`agent_provision\` paths). - 5 remaining \`elizaSandboxService.shutdown()\` callers from non-route contexts (\`agent-managed-github\`, \`agent-managed-discord\`, \`eliza-managed-launch\`, \`active-billing\`). Architectural question: should those services run as Workers or daemon? Same Worker-side gap applies.

Greptile Summary

This PR shifts all agent lifecycle operations (suspend, resume, restart, logs, snapshot) from inline Cloudflare Worker calls to a job-queue pattern executed by the daemon — the only context with SSH access to Hetzner cores. It adds five new job types mirroring the shipped `agent_delete` pattern, rewrites six route handlers to return `202 + jobId`, and implements five daemon-side `execute*` methods in `ElizaSandboxService`. - **Six route rewrites** across `v1/`, `v1/eliza/`, and `compat/` replace direct `elizaSandboxService` calls with `provisioningJobService.enqueue*Once` + `triggerImmediate()`, returning `202` and a polling envelope. - **Five new `execute*` methods** in `eliza-sandbox.ts` handle the actual SSH operations daemon-side: `executeSuspend` (advisory-locked transaction), `executeResume` (delegates to `provision()`), `executeRestart` (sequential `shutdown` + `provision`), `executeLogs` (SSH `docker logs`), and `executeSnapshot` (delegates to existing `snapshot()`). - **`DockerSandboxProvider.fetchLogs`** is added with integer-clamped `safeTail` and `2>&1` stderr merge so crashed agents' boot traces are included.

Confidence Score: 4/5

The structural change is sound — six routes correctly shifted from inline Worker calls to job-queue dispatched by the daemon — but the `triggerImmediate()` calls without CF Worker env bindings mean the orchestrator kick is a no-op at runtime, so jobs sit pending until the next cron tick. The `triggerImmediate` / missing env-binding issue carried from the prior review is a present runtime defect: the daemon wake-up call silently fails in every new route that adds the pattern, so suspend, restart, logs, and snapshot operations will always incur the full cron-tick latency rather than the near-instant dispatch intended. The `triggerImmediate()` calls across the new route handlers (restart, suspend, logs in `v1/agents/`, PATCH in `v1/eliza/agents/[agentId]/route.ts`, and the newly added resume/snapshot/suspend under `v1/eliza/agents/`) all need the `c.env` pass-through addressed before deploy.

Important Files Changed

| Filename | Overview | |----------|----------| | packages/cloud-shared/src/lib/services/provisioning-jobs.ts | Core of the PR: adds five new enqueue methods and five executor methods following the existing agent_delete/provision pattern. The `enqueueAgentSnapshotOnce` idempotency check doesn't filter by `snapshotType`, mirroring the already-flagged `tail`/logs issue. | | packages/cloud-shared/src/lib/services/eliza-sandbox.ts | Adds five daemon-side execute* methods. `executeSuspend` is properly transaction-locked; `executeResume` and `executeRestart` rely on provision()'s own lock but are not wrapped in a single atomic boundary (already flagged in previous review). | | packages/cloud-api/v1/agents/[agentId]/restart/route.ts | Route correctly enqueues AGENT_RESTART and returns 202; `triggerImmediate()` called without `c.env` (already flagged in previous review). | | packages/cloud-api/v1/eliza/agents/[agentId]/snapshot/route.ts | Enqueues AGENT_SNAPSHOT and returns 202; pre-gates on agent.status === "running". The idempotency check in the underlying enqueue method ignores snapshotType. | | packages/cloud-api/v1/agents/[agentId]/suspend/route.ts | Route enqueues AGENT_SUSPEND with correct idempotency fast-paths for stopped/provisioning. `triggerImmediate()` missing `c.env` (already flagged). | | packages/cloud-api/v1/agents/[agentId]/logs/route.ts | Route enqueues AGENT_LOGS; `triggerImmediate()` missing `c.env` (already flagged). Idempotency ignores `tail` (already flagged). | | packages/cloud-api/v1/eliza/agents/[agentId]/route.ts | PATCH handler now enqueues AGENT_SUSPEND; `triggerImmediate()` missing `c.env` in the new path (already flagged), whereas DELETE correctly passes c.env. | | packages/cloud-api/v1/eliza/agents/[agentId]/resume/route.ts | Switches from enqueueAgentProvisionOnce to enqueueAgentResumeOnce; drops expectedUpdatedAt check. Clean change, no issues found. | | packages/cloud-api/v1/eliza/agents/[agentId]/suspend/route.ts | Correctly rewired to enqueue AGENT_SUSPEND with proper 409/already-stopped fast-paths. Clean implementation. | | packages/cloud-api/compat/agents/[id]/logs/route.ts | Compat logs route rewired to job queue; shares the same `triggerImmediate()` without env context issue as the v1 routes (same pattern flagged in previous review). | | packages/cloud-api/compat/agents/[id]/suspend/route.ts | Compat suspend route correctly handles stopped/provisioning fast-paths and enqueues AGENT_SUSPEND with 202 response. | | packages/cloud-shared/src/lib/services/docker-sandbox-provider.ts | Adds fetchLogs with integer-clamped tail (1-5000), shellQuote protection, and stderr merge via 2>&1. Clean implementation. | | packages/cloud-shared/src/lib/services/provisioning-job-types.ts | Adds five new job type constants; tested for uniqueness and snake_case convention in the new provisioning-job-types.test.ts. | | packages/cloud-shared/src/lib/services/__tests__/provisioning-job-types.test.ts | New smoke tests validate the job type registry for uniqueness, snake_case format, and correct wire values. Good coverage for the registry file. |

Sequence Diagram

```mermaid sequenceDiagram participant C as Client participant W as CF Worker (route) participant DB as Database (jobs) participant D as Daemon (orchestrator) participant H as Hetzner Core (SSH) C->>W: "POST /suspend | /restart | /snapshot" W->>DB: "enqueue*Once() advisory lock, idempotency check, INSERT job" DB-->>W: "job {id, status: pending}" W->>D: triggerImmediate() best-effort W-->>C: "202 {jobId, polling.endpoint}" Note over D: Cron tick or triggerImmediate kick D->>DB: SELECT pending jobs DB-->>D: job row alt AGENT_SUSPEND or AGENT_RESTART D->>H: SSH docker stop container H-->>D: ok D->>DB: "UPDATE status=stopped, bridge_url=NULL" else AGENT_RESUME D->>H: provision() docker run + health check H-->>D: bridgeUrl, healthUrl D->>DB: "UPDATE status=running, bridge_url, health_url" else AGENT_LOGS D->>H: SSH docker logs --tail N H-->>D: stdout+stderr else AGENT_SNAPSHOT D->>H: fetch bridge_url/snapshot H-->>D: backup metadata D->>DB: INSERT agent_sandbox_backups end D->>DB: "UPDATE jobs SET status=completed, result" C->>DB: "GET /api/v1/jobs/{id} poll until completed" DB-->>C: "{status, result}" ```

Comments Outside Diff (1)

 1. `packages/cloud-shared/src/lib/services/provisioning-jobs.ts`, line 936-957 ([link](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/blob/e8f60f9765a3bf8a44848d297701dcd89642909c/packages/cloud-shared/src/lib/services/provisioning-jobs.ts#L936-L957)) **Idempotency key for `AGENT_LOGS` ignores the `tail` parameter** The in-flight dedup query matches any `pending`/`in_progress` `agent_logs` job for the same agent, regardless of `tail`. A second request for `tail=5000` while a `tail=100` job is executing returns the `tail=100` job, but both route handlers respond with the caller's own `tail` value in the envelope. The client sees `alreadyInProgress: true` but the job stored in `jobs.data.tail` and eventually written to `jobs.result.tail` is 100 — clients polling the job will receive 100-line logs when they asked for 5000. Either include `tail` in the idempotency check, or make the response tail field come from the returned job's data rather than the caller's parameter.
Reviews (2): Last reviewed commit: ["chore(cloud-shared): biome format drift ..."](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/commit/3f873db40c04d2824a6b5e9e2d5d43ea237f69e2) | [Re-trigger Greptile](https://app.greptile.com/api/retrigger?id=32895524)

topPRs.title: fix(android): wire local MTP and OmniVoice inference
topPRs.author: NubsCarson
topPRs.body:
## Summary

This PR wires the Android local-inference path to the current Shaw `llama.cpp` fork contract on `develop`:

- builds and packages the real Android speculative shim against `llama.cpp` headers/libs instead of the previous headerless shim path
- stages `libllama-common.so` and other DT_NEEDED siblings required by the speculative shim and merged server artifacts
- exposes `eliza_llama_context_params_set_ctx_type(...)` so mobile can request `LLAMA_CONTEXT_TYPE_MTP`
- adds MTP-first / DFlash-fallback model-load selection for the AOSP local inference adapter
- validates DFlash metadata before auto-pairing a drafter with a target model
- routes Android local TTS through stock-safe Kokoro by default, with OmniVoice auto/explicit support and fallback behavior
- bundles the mobile-safe OmniVoice/plugin-workflow surfaces needed by the Android agent bundle
- fixes the OmniVoice FFI pointer write path for numeric and bigint pointers

There is also one formatter-only commit for three homepage files from the latest `develop` merge, because the repo-wide format CI currently checks the full workspace.

## Current Source Base

- `elizaOS/eliza` base merged: `develop` at `d750978dc7`
- `plugins/plugin-local-inference/native/llama.cpp`: `3de6dc888a5fbccdb989b8b7be87d42c8ad0c925` (`v1.0.1-eliza-748-g3de6dc888`)

## Validation

Host checks run locally on 2026-05-19:

- `git diff --cached --check` and `git diff --check`
- `node --check packages/agent/scripts/build-mobile-bundle.mjs`
- `node --check packages/agent/scripts/mobile-stubs/null-plugin.cjs`
- `node --check packages/app-core/scripts/aosp/compile-libllama.mjs`
- `bun test packages/app-core/scripts/aosp/compile-libllama-fused.test.mjs --runInBand` (`12 pass`)
- `bun run typecheck` in `plugins/plugin-aosp-local-inference`
- `bun run test` in `plugins/plugin-aosp-local-inference` (`49 pass`)
- `bun run typecheck` in `plugins/plugin-omnivoice`
- `bun run test` in `plugins/plugin-omnivoice` (`28 pass`, `1 skipped`)
- `bun run format:check` in `packages/homepage`
- Android fused native dry-run against the in-repo `3de6dc888` submodule

Full native Android build also passed with:

```sh
node packages/app-core/scripts/aosp/compile-libllama.mjs \
  --target android-arm64-cpu-fused \
  --src-dir plugins/plugin-local-inference/native/llama.cpp \
  --assets-dir /home/nubs/.eliza/local-inference/bin/dflash/apk-android-arm64-cpu-fused-shawlatest-3de6dc888 \
  --jobs 8
```

That build produced `libllama.so`, `libllama-common.so`, `libmtmd.so`, `libelizainference.so`, `libomnivoice.so`, `llama-server`, `libeliza-llama-shim.so`, and `libeliza-llama-speculative-shim.so`. Artifact checks found Qwen35/Qwen35MoE/Qwen3Next/DFlash symbols, MTP markers, and the QJL/Polar/TBQ/fused-attention kernels.

A Milady wrapper APK build using those artifacts also completed successfully and packaged the native set into the debug APK.

## Hardware Caveat

This is intentionally a draft because there is no Pixel device available right now. I am not claiming on-device chat latency, audio quality, NNAPI/TPU behavior, or end-to-end voice conversation proof from this PR. The host/native/APK build evidence is real; final Pixel validation still needs hardware.

completedItems.title: fix(boot): eliminate TLA + barrel-import deadlock in cloud Docker boot
completedItems.body:
## Why

Provisioning a fresh sandbox in a cloud Docker container hangs silently: no logs past `[eliza] Generated SECRET_SALT`, Node PID 1 in `ep_poll`, port 2138 never listens, daemon kills the container at the 180s health timeout. PR #7783
